title: FREN and BBRI are foreign investors’ favourites
body: FREN and BBRI were at the top of the net buy list until the close of trading on Wednesday (15/1).

title: QRIS transaction volume saw 175.2% surge in 2024
body: Digital payments in 2024 recorded a total of 34.5 billion transactions, growing by 36.1% year-on-year (yoy).

title: BI rate drops to 5.75%
body: BI has lowered the BI rate to 5.75% from the previous 6%, taking into account the inflation forecast for 2025 and 2026.

title: Bond payment deferred, PPRO suspension extended
body: The suspension follows the payment delay of the 11th coupon of its Sustainable Bond II Phase IV Year 2022 Series B, amounting to IDR 4.33 billion, to bondholders.

title: Total imports rose by 5.31% throughout 2024
body: BPS recorded an increase in total imports throughout 2024 compared to 2023, with the rise coming from both the oil and gas and non-oil and gas sectors.

title: Suspension lifted, UANG saw corrections
body: UANG was corrected by IDR 25 to IDR 600 per share only one day after Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) lifted its suspension.

title: RATU listed under UMA after 5-days of ARA, now overvalued
body: This price surge brings RATU’s P/E Ratio to 34.06, much higher compared to the average of energy and oil-and-gas companies.

title: Research: ARPU dropped, are telecommunication stocks still attractive?
body: Slowed-down demand and fierce competition are said to be the factor of ARPU's dip of up to 5% yoy in the telecommunication sector.

title: Oil tanker rates surge after US sanction on Russia
body: Importers to seek out other suppliers following the sanction given by the United States (US) government to Russia.

title: JCI down 0.86%, lowest in 7 months
body: Shares of big caps dragged down JCI, falling to the lowest level in 7 months.

title: Foreign sales volume remains high; net sell at 832 million shares
body: A total of 2.47 billion shares were purchased and 3.31 billion shares were sold by foreign investors on Tuesday, January 14.

title: Conditions met, Yonden is close to officially acquire HGII
body: The acquisition was carried out over 1,62 billion shares, with price set to IDR 200 each, thus bringing total investment value to IDR 325 billion.

title: Amar Bank's embedded banking now available on JULO
body: The official statement mentioned that the credit facility available to JULO users could reach up to IDR 50 million.

title: OJK urges financial institutions to ease mortgage loans for low-income families
body: OJK is encouraging financial service institutions to facilitate the provision of housing ownership loans (KPR) for low-income households.

title: DBS Bank Ltd divests 23.20 million shares of PZZA
body: DBS Bank Ltd, one of PZZA's shareholders, divested its portion in a transaction last week (7/1).

title: Medco extends IDR 7 trillion loan to subsidiary
body: Despite the significant amount, MEDC announced that the loan would not bear any interest.

title: Remaining IPO proceeds of CMRY still parked in deposits and bonds
body: Data from the Indonesia Stock Exchange (IDX) disclosure shows that this utilization represents only 38.58% of the total IPO proceeds.

title: INCO assigns Retno Marsudi as Independent Commissioner
body: Retno Lestari Priansari Marsudi, former Minister of Foreign Affairs, is named as Independent Commissioner of INCO until 2027.

title: Siloam declares interest in acquiring First REIT hospitals
body: SILO is currently the existing tenant and operator of all First REIT hospitals in Indonesia.

title: CLEO aims for double-digit growth again this year
body: The strategy to be implemented includes new factory, distribution network enhancement, and operational efficiency through integrated production process.

title: Multipolar sells 37.50 million shares of Multipolar Technology (MLPT)
body: Once this transaction was completed through PT Ciptadana Sekuritas Asia, the controller saw a drop in shares from about 87% to 85%.

title: NINE to branch out to cyber security sector this year
body: NINE) will expand its services to cyber security and e-catalogue to boost revenue in 2025.

title: Danai.id and J Trust Bank synergise for blue economy
body: One type of this partnership includes inventory financing facility, a facility that utilises inventory stock as collaterals.

title: Morning session, JCI saw climbs
body: JCI is showing uptrend this morning (14/1) at the first trading session.

title: Antam to join JIIPE, planning smelter in Gresik
body: The smelter of PT Freeport Indonesia (FPI) at JIIPE will give Antam another leverage.

title: PTRO potentially joins hands with Happy Hapsoro’s RATU
body: The potential collaboration was rumoured after detecting an affiliation link between the ultimate controlling shareholders of PTRO and RATU.

title: Oil price climbed 2%, highest in the past 4 months
body: Global oil price is predicted to remain high, targeted at between USD 70 and USD 85 per barrel.

title: Bumi Resources Minerals’ boss bag 2.32 million BRMS’s shares
body: BRMS's shares were acquired by the director for personal investment.

title: Dropping 1.02%, BBRI and BREN shares drove down JCI
body: BBRI, BREN, and ASII drove down JCI performance in yesterday's trade.

title: Foreign trades decline; net sell at 103.72 million share
body: Foreign investors sold 2.99 billion shares of stock on Monday, January 9, which was a decrease from the previous stock exchange day. Approximately 2.88 billion shares were purchased.

title: KIJA leads early week Net Buy
body: KIJA and BIPI were at the top of the net foreign buy leaderboard on the stock exchange at the close of Monday's trading (13/1)

title: OBAT targets 20% growth, eyeing Free Nutritious Meal program
body: "We have held several discussions regarding this free lunch program, and we are just waiting for final information from the National Nutrition Agency."

title: RGAS realises IDR 35.18 billion of IPO proceeds
body: RGAS realised IDR35.18 billion or 99.9% of the net proceeds from the IPO of IDR35.19 billion on November 8, 2023.

title: 468 million Impack Pratama (IMPC) shares acquired by Harimas Tunggal Perkasa
body: Following the completion of the purchase of these shares through PT Verdhana Sekuritas Indonesia, the investor's share percentage grew to 19.73%.

title: Another lay-off wave to hit, now it’s Bukalapak
body: BUKA is set to terminate a number of employees following its business shift to focus exclusively on virtual product services.
